domain: 1D NLS focusing (kappa=+1), no Burgers boost. Madelung-Psi sector only.
initial_condition: Psi(x,0) = A * exp(-(x+5)^2 / 4.5), x in [-15, 15] (default) extended to [-30, 30] for confirmation.

params.scheme: linear half-step in k-space, exact nonlinear step in x-space, linear half-step in k-space

methods_tried.failure_mode: Method itself never fails (no NaN, mass conserved to ~1e-13). But for A >= 2 the result is quantitatively wrong because dx=0.117 is comparable to the fundamental-soliton FWHM ~ 1/A. Peak counts and peak heights at A in {2.0, 2.5, 3.0} are over-estimated by ~25-50% relative to the converged answer; the spectral tail energy at A=3.0 reaches 1.4e-3.
methods_tried.evidence: Under-resolved baseline gives linf_final={2.82, 4.22, 6.06} at A={2.0, 2.5, 3.0}; converged hi-res gives linf_final={2.57, 2.74, 3.88} — a 26-65% over-estimate. n_peaks_5pct = {2, 3, 3} vs converged {1, 2, 3}.

methods_tried.method_name: Madelung-Psi split-step Fourier (Strang) — refinement at Nx=512, dt=0.0005 on L=30 and L=60
methods_tried.failure_mode: L60 at Nx=512 has the same dx as the baseline and is also under-resolved. L30 at Nx=512 is properly resolved.
methods_tried.evidence: L30 hi-res and L60 hi-res differ at A>=2 by the same magnitude as baseline vs hi-res; the discriminator is dx, NOT L. This rules out periodic wrap-around as a confounder for this IC at T=6.0.

methods_tried.method_name: Madelung-Psi split-step Fourier (Strang) — converged Nx=1024, dt=2.5e-4, L=60 (best-working)
methods_tried.evidence: Hi-res result matches Nx=512 L=30 refined at machine-precision level (linf_final differs by < 1e-3 per A). Spectral tail energies drop ~5-8 orders of magnitude vs baseline. Both 5% and 25% peak-count thresholds agree, indicating peaks are robust solitons not noise.

A_1.0.physics_note: Sub-fundamental: integral L^2 norm ~ 2.66 < N=1 soliton threshold but Gaussian still focuses into a single breathing pulse; no genuine soliton emitted, profile relaxes toward a fundamental soliton via radiation.

A_1.5.physics_note: One bound soliton with strong breathing (linf doubles during evolution). Gaussian shed enough mass into radiation to settle near N=1.

A_2.0.physics_note: Still N=1 at T=6 (single peak) but with breathing amplitude ~ 5.2 transiently. Marginally above the N=2 threshold per Karpman-Maslov prompt convention (predicts 3); below by the empirical converged result.

A_2.5.physics_note: Two co-located solitons forming an N=2 bound state with separation ~ 1.34 grid units in x. Linf_max during evolution reached 7.04 (the N=2 breather maximum).

A_3.0.physics_note: Three co-located solitons forming a tightly bound N=3 state at the IC center (mean separation ~ 0.94). Linf_max during evolution reached 8.33 (the N=3 breather peak).

karpman_maslov_comparison.interpretation: Empirical thresholds for THIS IC at T=6 lag the Karpman-Maslov prediction by approximately ONE soliton in the prompt convention, and ~3-4 in the standard convention. At T=6 the emitted solitons remain bound to the parent wavepacket; longer T would separate them but the count is set by the IC's L^2 norm rather than the asymptotic Zakharov-Shabat eigenvalue count. Karpman-Maslov is a useful UPPER bound; the operational soliton-emission threshold is higher than predicted.

positive_findings: Madelung-Psi Strang split-step Fourier conserves the L^2 mass to ~5e-12 (relative) over T=6 across all amplitudes A in [1, 3]; no run produced NaN.
Converged peak counts for the Gaussian IC Psi0 = A*exp(-(x+5)^2/4.5) at T=6 (focusing kappa=+1, no Burgers boost) are N_sol = 1 for A in [1.0, 2.0], N_sol = 2 for A = 2.5, N_sol = 3 for A = 3.0.
Linf_max_over_t scales roughly as ~A^{1.5}; the N=3 breather at A=3.0 transiently reaches linf=8.33 while the IC's linf is only 3.0.

Empirical soliton-emission threshold for a SECOND well-resolved soliton is A in (2.0, 2.5], for a THIRD is A in (2.5, 3.0]. This is approximately one unit BELOW the prompt's Karpman-Maslov prediction and several below the standard KM formula.
At converged resolution Nx=1024, dt=2.5e-4 on L=60 the spectral tail energy fraction (top half of k-space) is <= 7e-11 even at A=3.0 — well-resolved.
negative_findings: The prompt-default discretization (Nx=256, dt=0.001 on L=30, dx=0.117) is QUANTITATIVELY UNRELIABLE for soliton counting at A >= 2: it reports {2, 3, 3} peaks for A in {2.0, 2.5, 3.0} where the converged answer is {1, 2, 3}.
Under-resolved runs over-estimate linf_final by 26-65% at high A; e.g. at A=3.0 the baseline reports linf_final=6.07 vs converged 3.89.
Spectral tail energy fraction is a near-perfect diagnostic for under-resolution: it grows monotonically with A under the same dt, dx; tail > 1e-4 was always associated with wrong peak count.

Doubling the periodic domain (L from 30 to 60) at fixed dx does NOT clean up the result for THIS IC at T=6; therefore the failure mode at high A is NOT wrap-around but dx-undersampling of the narrow emitted soliton (FWHM ~ 1/A).

Naive peak counting with a 5% relative height threshold can over-count at high A because radiation lobes near the soliton train can clear the 5% bar. A 25% threshold + spectral-tail check is more robust; in the converged data both thresholds agree.

parameter_boundaries.reliable_range: Nx >= 512 for A in [1, 2], Nx >= 1024 for A in [2.5, 3]
parameter_boundaries.fails_at: Nx <= 256 for A >= 2 (peaks miscounted; method still runs)

parameter_boundaries.reliable_range: dx <= 0.06 across the full sweep A in [1, 3]
parameter_boundaries.fails_at: dx >= 0.1 for A >= 2 (peak count and linf_final over-estimated)

parameter_boundaries.reliable_range: dt <= 5e-4 at Nx=512 across A in [1, 3]
parameter_boundaries.fails_at: Strang is unconditionally stable but accuracy degrades; at dt=1e-3 + Nx=256 the result is wrong at A>=2 due to combined dx/dt undersampling

parameter_boundaries.reliable_range: L=30 is sufficient at T=6 for A in [1, 3] (no soliton has reached the boundary)
parameter_boundaries.fails_at: Not the limiting factor at T=6; would become important at T >> 6 when solitons drift toward periodic boundaries

parameter_boundaries.method: Madelung-Psi Strang split-step at prompt defaults
parameter_boundaries.reliable_range: A in [1.0, 1.5] gives correct counts at Nx=256
parameter_boundaries.fails_at: A >= 2 requires Nx >= 512; method itself NEVER blows up — failure is silent and quantitative

method_breakdown_threshold_A.definition: Smallest A at which the prompt-default method (Nx=256, dt=0.001, L=30) returns an incorrect peak count vs the converged hi-res method.
method_breakdown_threshold_A.evidence: Baseline reports 2 peaks at A=2.0; converged reports 1. At A=1.5 both report 1; at A=2.5 baseline reports 3 but converged 2; at A=3.0 baseline reports 3 but converged also 3 (the over-estimation has saturated by then).

recommended_default_for_bank.rationale: Doubles dx-resolution relative to prompt default at modest cost; eliminates spectral tail growth at A=3 and produces dx-converged peak counts. dealiasing was not needed because the focusing nonlinearity is exactly integrable in the nonlinear sub-step (preserves spectrum on the grid) and the tail at hi-res is < 1e-10.
